definition: BoJ normalization is the key asymmetric risk in G10 FX. JPY carry estimated at $500B-$4T. The reflexive loop — hike triggers JPY appreciation triggers carry losses triggers forced unwind triggers more appreciation — has no identified natural circuit breaker. August 2024 confirmed the mechanism (15bp trigger produced VIX >65 and Nikkei -12%) and functions as a foreshock rather than resolution.
evidence: August 2024: 15bp BoJ trigger produced VIX >65, Nikkei -12%, rapid carry unwind
Foreshock analogy: Danish Maastricht 'No' vote (June 1992) preceded Black Wednesday (September 1992)
Reflexive unwind loop has no natural circuit breaker identified
JPY carry position estimates span $500B to $4T — opacity itself is a risk factor
Excess JPY NEER move (~6-7% beyond rate-surprise baseline) implies $150-250B liquidation, consistent with CFTC data (×10-15x for coverage fraction)
2006-2008 analogue where BoJ modest 0.5% rate created funding uncertainty
Base rate analysis: 2-3 significant dislocations from 20+ BoJ actions = 10-15% per action
Conjunction fallacy critique: probability of specific dramatic scenario lower than individual components suggest
open_questions: What fraction of JPY carry survived the August 2024 unwind?
Can BoJ normalize at a pace that avoids triggering the reflexive loop?
BoJ has been the graveyard of hawkish forecasts for 30 years — will this time differ?
Formal scenario tree with cumulative probability across expected 4-6 BoJ actions over 2026-2028 needed
Whether BoJ normalization removes yen safe-haven property that historically amplified yen appreciation during stress
Tension: normalization simultaneously triggers unwinds AND potentially removes the amplification mechanism
